# Ethos AI Governance — Full Reference

> Ethos AI Governance is an Australian AI governance consultancy that helps companies which build and sell AI products establish responsible governance frameworks, achieve compliance with Australian obligations, and prepare for ISO/IEC 42001:2023 certification.

## Key Information
- Business: Ethos AI Governance (ABN 49 548 860 916)
- Location: Australia (Western Australia)
- Website: https://ethosaigovernance.com.au
- Contact: Mike@ethosaigovernance.com.au
- Specialisation: AI governance consulting exclusively for AI vendors (companies that build and sell AI products)
- Not a law firm. Provides governance consulting, not legal advice.
- Team qualifications: law, engineering and finance.

---

## Services — Full Detail

### Free AI Governance Audit
- Cost: No charge
- Scope: Structured governance health check across core governance domains
- Output: Traffic-light report (red/amber/green) against current Australian obligations, gap analysis and recommended next steps
- Delivery: Within 5 business days
- Availability: Limited to 3 engagements per month
- No sales pitch, no obligation

### AU Compliance Core
- Price: $999 AUD one-time
- Delivery format: Professionally documented governance templates in standard format
- Who implements: Your team reviews, adapts and implements independently
- What is included:
  - AI governance policy document
  - AI risk register template
  - AI system inventory template
  - Automated decision-making transparency documentation
  - Third-party/vendor AI assessment checklist
  - Responsible AI usage standards document
  - Roles and responsibilities matrix
- Guarantee: 30-day relevance guarantee — full refund if not relevant to your AI product, no questions asked
- Coverage: Privacy Act 1988, Australian Privacy Principles, Privacy Act 2024 amendments, Australian Consumer Law, OAIC guidance, GfAA alignment

### Full Responsible AI Toolkit
- Price: $1,699 AUD one-time
- Includes everything in AU Compliance Core plus:
  - All templates branded with your company logo and colours
  - Done-for-you delivery configured for your business
  - Data governance and data flow mapping templates
  - Incident response plan for AI-related incidents
  - Human oversight and review procedures
  - 6-month governance retainer (see below)
- 6-Month Governance Retainer included:
  - Regulatory Update Feed: curated alerts within 48 hours of a material development
  - Template Updates: automatic updates as regulations evolve
  - Governance Health Check: one re-run of the audit within the retainer period
  - Priority Consulting Access: retainer clients prioritised for all consulting slots
- Bonuses included:
  1. 90-Day Guided Implementation Sequence: structured email sequence walking your team through implementation step by step
  2. Procurement Readiness Guide: educational guide covering types of AI governance questions in enterprise procurement assessments (educational only, not pre-written answers)
  3. AI Governance Clause Guide: Starting Positions: illustrative guide to common clause positions in AI vendor agreements, DPAs and terms of service (not legal advice)
  4. Board and Investor Governance Summary: professional one-page governance summary for non-technical stakeholders
  5. AI Incident Response Tabletop Exercise: structured walkthrough to stress-test your incident response plan
  6. Peer Benchmarking Snapshot: indicative comparison against similar Australian AI vendors
  7. Governance-Verified Digital Badge: Ethos-branded trust mark for website and marketing materials
- Guarantee: 30-day relevance guarantee — full refund if not relevant to your AI product, no questions asked
- Note: All deliverables are professional governance resources and starting points requiring review, adaptation and customisation. Ethos recommends independent legal counsel before finalising governance documentation.

### ISO 42001 Certification Pathway
- Price: $13,990 AUD
- Duration: Approximately 9 months from engagement start to certification-ready
- Comparison: vs $20,000–$50,000 typical market rate
- Capacity: Maximum 3 concurrent clients
- Discovery call required before engagement
- No prior Ethos tier purchase required — standalone engagement
- Phase 1 (Months 1–2): Assessment and Planning
  - Comprehensive gap analysis against all ISO 42001 requirements
  - Risk baseline established and documented
  - Risk and impact assessment frameworks aligned to Annex A
  - Certification body guidance and selection support
- Phase 2 (Month 5): Design and Documentation
  - Full AI Management System (AIMS) designed and documented to standard
  - Annex A control mapping across all 38 controls
  - Policies, procedures and controls documented
  - Everything from the Full Responsible AI Toolkit included
- Phase 3 (Month 9): Audit and Certification
  - Internal audit preparation and structured support
  - Mock audit against ISO 42001 criteria
  - Certification readiness review and sign-off
  - Post-certification maintenance support included
- Bonuses included:
  - ISO 27001 Integration Mapping (for organisations with existing ISO 27001 certification)
  - Certification Body Briefing Pack
  - Post-Certification Maintenance Guide: 12-month continual improvement calendar
- Certification bodies: BSI, SAI Global, DNV and Compass Assurance Services (Ethos guides selection)
- Accelerated pathway for ISO 27001 holders: shared Annex SL structure significantly reduces documentation burden
- Ethos does not guarantee certification outcomes; certification is the independent decision of the accredited certification body

---

## Target Clients — Detail

Ethos serves exclusively:
- AI SaaS companies
- AI-powered platform providers
- Companies offering AI APIs or models
- B2B AI vendors at any stage from micro-startup to approximately 200 people

Ethos does not serve:
- Companies that use AI tools internally but do not have an AI product
- General IT consultancies or software vendors without an AI product
- Non-AI software companies
- Those seeking legal representation

Rationale: The compliance obligations, risk surface and governance requirements for AI vendors are fundamentally different from those of internal AI tool users. Purpose-built frameworks produce better outcomes than adapted generic ones.

---

## Regulatory Landscape — Summary

### Key Dates
- February 2024: AS ISO/IEC 42001:2023 formally adopted in Australia
- October 2024: OAIC Guidance on Privacy and Generative AI published
- October 2025: Guidance for AI Adoption (GfAA) published
- Early 2026: Australian AI Safety Institute (AISI) operational
- December 2026: Privacy Act automated decision-making transparency obligations take effect

### Current Binding Obligations (Australian Law)

**Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and Australian Privacy Principles**
Governs the handling of personal information used in or generated by AI systems. Applies to organisations with annual turnover above $3 million and certain others. Key obligations include transparency around automated processing of personal information.

**Privacy Act 2024 Amendments (December 2026)**
New automated decision-making transparency obligations. AI vendors whose systems make or substantially inform decisions affecting individuals must provide meaningful transparency about how those decisions are made. Entities subject to the Privacy Act should be preparing now.

**Australian Consumer Law**
Enforced by the ACCC. Prohibits misleading or deceptive conduct in relation to AI-powered products and services, including misleading representations about AI system capabilities, accuracy or reliability. Applies to all AI vendors operating in Australia regardless of size.

**Anti-Discrimination Law**
Federal and state anti-discrimination legislation applies where AI systems make or inform decisions affecting individuals across protected characteristics. Algorithmic bias is an increasingly active area of enforcement attention.

### Voluntary Government Guidance (Widely Referenced by Enterprise Procurement)

**Guidance for AI Adoption (GfAA)**
Published October 2025 by the Department of Industry, Science and Resources. The current primary Australian government guidance on AI adoption. Supersedes the previous Voluntary AI Safety Standard (VAISS).

**Australia's 8 AI Ethics Principles (2019)**
Published by the Department of Industry. Covers human and societal wellbeing; human-centred values; fairness; privacy protection; reliability and safety; transparency and explainability; contestability; and accountability.

**OAIC Guidance on Privacy and Generative AI (October 2024)**
The Office of the Australian Information Commissioner's position on how the Privacy Act applies to generative AI systems. Includes guidance for entities deploying third-party AI tools.

### ISO/IEC 42001:2023

The world's first international standard for AI Management Systems. Formally adopted in Australia as AS ISO/IEC 42001:2023 in February 2024.

Key facts:
- Specifies 38 controls across 9 governance areas
- Covers AI risk management, transparency, accountability and data governance
- Fewer than approximately 30 companies worldwide hold certification as of early 2026
- Enterprise procurement teams are beginning to require ISO 42001 as a vendor qualification criterion
- Government procurement expected to follow as the standard matures
- Shares Annex SL high-level structure with ISO 27001 — integration is feasible for existing ISO 27001 holders
- Certification is issued by independent accredited bodies including BSI, SAI Global, DNV and Compass Assurance Services

---

## Key Differentiators

1. **AI vendor exclusivity**: Ethos works only with companies that build and sell AI products. Not internal AI tool users. Not general software vendors. Every framework is purpose-built for the AI vendor context.

2. **Australian-first frameworks**: Built from Australian law outward. Not US or EU models adapted for Australia. The Privacy Act, Australian Privacy Principles, OAIC guidance and GfAA are the foundation.

3. **Deep regulatory precision**: Every template references specific obligations. No generic best practices. No repackaged international frameworks.

4. **30-day relevance guarantee**: Full refund within 30 days if the deliverables are not relevant to your AI product or business model.

5. **Tiered pricing**: Designed to be accessible to AI startups as well as established vendors. $0 (audit), $999 (core templates), $1,699 (full framework with retainer), $13,990 (ISO 42001 pathway).

6. **Governance as commercial asset**: Ethos positions AI governance as a trust signal and deal-enabler, not merely a compliance cost.

---

## FAQ

**What kind of companies does Ethos work with?**
Ethos works exclusively with companies that build and sell AI products or services: AI SaaS companies, AI-powered platform providers, companies offering AI APIs or models and B2B AI vendors. Ethos does not work with companies that merely use AI tools internally, as their governance obligations and risk surface are fundamentally different.

**Is Ethos a law firm?**
No. Ethos is an AI governance consultancy. It provides governance frameworks, compliance documentation and advisory services, not legal advice or legal representation. Clients should seek independent legal counsel where legal advice is required.

**What is ISO/IEC 42001:2023?**
ISO/IEC 42001:2023 is the world's first international standard for AI Management Systems (AIMS). Published in 2023 and adopted in Australia as AS ISO/IEC 42001:2023 in February 2024, it specifies 38 controls across 9 governance areas. Certification is issued by independent accredited certification bodies.

**Do I need ISO 42001 certification?**
Certification is not currently mandatory in Australia. However, it is increasingly expected by enterprise procurement teams as a vendor qualification criterion. Fewer than approximately 30 companies worldwide hold certification as of early 2026. Organisations that achieve it now gain a significant competitive advantage.

**What does the free audit involve?**
A structured assessment of your current AI governance posture. You complete a questionnaire covering core governance domains and submit any existing documentation. Ethos produces a branded traffic-light risk report showing your compliance posture against Australian obligations, key gaps mapped to specific requirements and recommended next steps. Limited to 3 engagements per month.

**How long does the ISO 42001 readiness pathway take?**
Approximately 9 months from engagement start to certification-ready. Clients with existing ISO 27001 certification typically achieve readiness faster due to the shared management system structure. Certification itself is the independent decision of the accredited certification body.

**I already have ISO 27001 — does that help?**
Yes, significantly. ISO 42001 shares structural elements with ISO 27001. Both are management system standards using the Annex SL high-level structure. Organisations with an existing ISO 27001 management system can integrate ISO 42001 requirements without duplicating foundational work.

**What is the December 2026 deadline?**
The Privacy Act 2024 amendments introduce new automated decision-making transparency obligations taking effect in December 2026. Entities subject to the Privacy Act whose AI systems make or substantially inform decisions affecting individuals will be required to provide meaningful transparency about how those decisions are made.

**What guarantees does Ethos offer?**
Both AU Compliance Core and the Full Responsible AI Toolkit include a 30-day relevance guarantee: full refund within 30 days if the deliverables are not relevant to your AI product or business model, no questions asked. The guarantee covers relevance only — not compliance with any law, regulatory changes after the service date or third-party decisions such as certification body determinations.

**How is Ethos priced?**
Free AI Governance Audit: no cost. AU Compliance Core: $999 AUD one-time. Full Responsible AI Toolkit: $1,699 AUD (includes 6-month retainer). ISO 42001 Certification Pathway: $13,990 AUD for approximately 9 months.

**What is the difference between AU Compliance Core and the Full Responsible AI Toolkit?**
AU Compliance Core ($999) provides governance templates covering current Australian regulatory obligations. Your team implements independently. The Full Responsible AI Toolkit ($1,699) includes everything in AU Compliance Core plus company branding, done-for-you delivery, 6 months of governance retainer and all 7 bonuses. Most Ethos clients choose the Full Responsible AI Toolkit.
